notes: Gravity for Dante's day at the helm. The boat groaning, the leaded window showing the dining room, the brass railing maintenance, Sammy's intercom-clicks, the catering phone, the card-room satchel. Endgame: Dean leaves the card with Sammy, Antonio's first-Sunday-of-even-month call comes through, the night closes with the ledger settled. Grounded reality: Dante is the office and the man holding the office is, by 60, mostly the office. The boat does not, today, speak; the boat groans because boats groan.

deck.title: the boat groans
deck.flavor: the boat shifts in its mooring. dante checked the levels last month with his father's spirit level. the boat is plumb. the listing is happening somewhere else.

deck.title: the brass railing is wrong
deck.flavor: you run a hand over the bright brass. the brass is the wrong brass; it has been the wrong brass since you installed it in the second year. you maintain it.

deck.title: the leaded window shows table 12
deck.flavor: through the architect's window. table 12 has the imperious wave. nicola is on it. she is on it the way nicola is on it.

deck.title: sammy pours for himself
deck.flavor: the first time since 1996. you see it from the helm. you do not, today, mention it. sammy will, you suspect, mention it.

deck.title: the catering phone rings
deck.flavor: downstairs. somebody in port arthur wants three hundred plates by october. the bride's father is somebody you did business with in '88. you remember the kind of business it was.

deck.title: mrs. karras laughed
deck.flavor: from the floor, the laugh. you have not, this round, gone down. somebody else got the laugh today. that is fine; the laugh exists.

deck.title: the card room runs friday
deck.flavor: saturday's regulars who came in tonight. the felt is original. the chips are racked. somebody is winning earlier than they should.

deck.title: the wife photograph
deck.flavor: on the wall opposite the desk. you have not replaced it in 23 years. you decided in the year after she died that the photograph you chose to keep up was the one you had been looking at when she was alive.

deck.title: alberto did not call
deck.flavor: again. he hasn't called in three months. you do not, today, call him first. you will, in some week, do it. you have not, yet, picked the week.

deck.title: the brass clock chimes
deck.flavor: from your father's office. since 1962. the chime is the chime. it has been the chime through three of your administrations.

deck.title: the intercom clicks
deck.flavor: the brass speaker on the desk. sammy testing the line. you press it. you say: i'm here, sammy. he says: just checking, mr. d. the line goes back to the line.

deck.title: the signage is peeling
deck.flavor: somebody mentioned it at the bar. you have not gone outside in eight months to look. the signage is a job for younger men.

deck.title: dean at table 14
deck.flavor: an out-of-town suit at fourteen. plain face. lapels-smoothing. drinking water. the practiced forgettableness of a man you have, perhaps a dozen times in your life, encountered. each of those meetings has cost you something later.

deck.title: the card arrives
deck.flavor: sammy at the office door, three short raps. plain stock, nothing embossed. mr. d. dean. consulting. dispute resolution and legacy planning. you set it square to the corner.

deck.title: the helm light goes off
deck.flavor: you reach for the chain. you turn it off. the office is dark. the boat is the boat. the ledger is the ledger. you walk down the iron stair the way you have walked it for sixty years.
